description: Provider-agnostic multi-agent project management: skills for Claude and Gemini + PTY overlay shell

tasks.title: Orchestrate skill — ledger-anchored rate cap derivation
tasks.description: Update the rate calibration logic in commands/orchestrate.md. Replace single-session delta method (cap = session_tokens / delta_pct) with ledger-anchored method: at session end, read agentflow_ledger.json, filter sessions where start_time >= window_start (local time; datetime.now() no tz), sum billable tokens (uncached_input + cache_creation + output per session), then cap_5hr = total_5hr_tokens / (end_pct_5hr / 100) and cap_wkly = total_wkly_tokens / (end_pct_wkly / 100). Window starts: 5hr = reset_time_5hr - 5h; weekly = reset_time_wkly - 7d. Gap: current session tokens not yet flushed to ledger — correct by adding (end_pct - start_pct) * prior_cap_estimate as current-session delta if ledger read returns fewer tokens than expected. Write updated cap estimates to ~/.agentflow/rate_calibration.json alongside existing EWMA fields.

unit: commands/orchestrate.md instructs ledger read at session end
commands/orchestrate.md filters sessions by start_time >= window_start (local time)
commands/orchestrate.md sums uncached_input + cache_creation + output per session
commands/orchestrate.md derives cap_5hr = total_5hr_tokens / (end_pct_5hr / 100)
commands/orchestrate.md derives cap_wkly = total_wkly_tokens / (end_pct_wkly / 100)
commands/orchestrate.md documents current-session gap and correction

tasks.acceptance_criteria: orchestrate.md rate calibration write uses ledger-anchored cap derivation; window start computation documented; current-session gap correction present

tasks.title: Oracle skill — general .idx reading protocol
tasks.description: Add a general Targeted Reads Rule section to commands/oracle.md. Currently oracle only checks .idx for architecture.md (Step 2a). All other file reads (lazy-loaded phase files: market.md 63L, checklist.md 113L, generation.md 133L; any future reads) have no .idx protocol. Add a 'Targeted Reads Rule' section matching the structure in commands/worker/system.md lines 51-78: compute HASH, check IDX path, grep for symbol, Read with offset+limit, fallback to full read if absent. Note: oracle's phase files (market.md, checklist.md, generation.md) are intentionally read in full when entering each phase — the rule applies to any targeted symbol/section lookup oracle makes outside of those full-phase loads.

unit: commands/oracle.md contains a Targeted Reads Rule section
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule instructs HASH computation
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule instructs grep for symbol in .idx
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule instructs Read with offset+limit
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule has fallback to full read when .idx absent

tasks.acceptance_criteria: oracle.md contains a general Targeted Reads Rule matching the worker protocol; existing Step 2a architecture.md-specific section preserved

tasks.title: PTY shell — I/O interception wrapper
tasks.description: Implement agentflow/shell/pty_wrapper.py. Class PTYWrapper(command: list, on_output=None, on_exit=None) matching existing stub. Methods: read_output(timeout: float = 1.0) -> bytes — reads available bytes from child PTY fd with timeout via select.select; write_input(text: str) -> None — writes UTF-8 encoded text to child PTY stdin fd. Constructor forks child under PTY using pty.fork(), stores master_fd and child_pid, sets fd non-blocking, registers SIGWINCH handler to forward terminal resize. on_output callback called with each bytes chunk from read_output. on_exit callback called with exit code when child exits. Raise PTYError on fork failure. stdlib-only: pty, os, select, signal, fcntl, termios.

unit: PTYError raised when pty.fork fails (mock pty.fork)
write_input encodes text as UTF-8 and writes to master_fd
read_output returns empty bytes on timeout
integration: wrap echo command — on_output callback receives output bytes

security_constraints: No shell=True — command must be list[str]
No LLM calls from this module
tasks.acceptance_criteria: PTYWrapper forks child under PTY, relays I/O via read_output/write_input, fires on_output and on_exit callbacks correctly

tasks.title: PTY session manager — handoff, countdown, idx injection
tasks.description: Implement agentflow/shell/session_manager.py and agentflow/shell/countdown.py. SessionManager(pty_wrapper, tokenizer, config) matching existing stub. Config is a dict with keys: oracle_threshold_tokens (60000), orchestrator_threshold_tokens (30000), threshold_pct (0.30), restart_delay_seconds (5). Method trigger_handoff() -> None: write '/handoff\n' to pty_wrapper.write_input(), scan on_output stream for 'HANDOFF_COMPLETE' (timeout 120s), write '/clear\n', call countdown(restart_delay_seconds, on_complete=restart_session). Additional responsibilities wired via on_output callback registered in __init__: (1) session_type detection — scan first chunks for '/oracle' or '/orchestrate' in stdin mirror; (2) turn counter — increment on response boundary; (3) idx reminder injection — every 3 turns call write_input(idx_banner) before next user input; idx_banner uses hash=hashlib.sha256(cwd).hexdigest() pre-computed at init; (4) threshold check — after each accumulate() call check total > threshold or total > window_size * threshold_pct; if fired and not manual_handoff: call trigger_handoff(). countdown.py: countdown(seconds: int, on_complete, message: str = '') -> None — prints per-second countdown to stderr, calls on_complete() when done; KeyboardInterrupt exits cleanly. Config read: open ~/.agentflow/config.toml via tomllib if present; merge over defaults.

unit: session_type set to 'oracle' when /oracle detected in input stream
session_type set to 'orchestrate' when /orchestrate detected
idx banner written via write_input every 3 turns
trigger_handoff() writes /handoff then /clear to pty_wrapper
threshold fires when tokenizer total exceeds oracle_threshold_tokens
manual_handoff flag suppresses auto-restart
countdown() prints per-second output and calls on_complete
countdown() exits cleanly on KeyboardInterrupt

security_constraints: config.toml path validated to ~/.agentflow/ before open
tasks.acceptance_criteria: SessionManager detects session type, injects idx banner every 3 turns, triggers handoff+countdown+restart when threshold exceeded; countdown callable works standalone
